site_evidence.site_label: Arabian Peninsula and Levant dromedary context
site_evidence.exact_source_text: The remains of a single large-sized Late Pleistocene camel individual recovered from the Site 1040 near Wadi Halfa were first evaluated by Gautier.
site_evidence.chronology_text: Historical and archaeological dromedary range context
site_evidence.interpretation_note: The paper names an exact Wadi Halfa specimen, but the current repository still publishes camel only as broad non-Nordic context rather than a precise atlas-grade point.
site_evidence.support_gap_note: The shipped point is still a broad contextual centroid even though the paper names Site 1040 near Wadi Halfa.
